target: HOL-HIERARCHY c2 — sub-hierarchy fixes for Part 2 internal ordering, three new SC subsections (κ-divide-by-zero, 1,660:1 ring-magnet, FSF version-unstable), V13 coordinate-system promotion, Part 1 tier classification update, advocate-mode preemption
short_recommendation: Six integration patches in priority order: P0 (renumber post-Summary loose args 2.10-2.12 + move Summary to true end-of-Part-2), P1-P3 (add three new SC subsections 2.13 κ-divide-by-zero, 2.14 ring-magnet 1,660:1, 2.15 FSF version-unstable), P4 (promote V13 coordinate from 2.9 to 2.1), P5 (update Part 1 tier classification to include 1.5/1.7/1.8/1.9 as Tier 1), P6 (Part 1.1 'case is overdetermined' acknowledgement). Two lower-priority advisory items (callout boxes on Parts 2/5/7 tabs; Part 5 rename) flagged for operator decision but not patched here.
kernel_of_truth: The V6 restructure (2026-04-07) substantially adopted the c1 recommendation: self-contradictions are now the first argument class after model description, with explicit 'why these come first' framing in Part 2 intro. The 3-tier signposting in Part 1's 'How This Review Is Organized' is a real improvement. The macrostructure is good — the c1 reviewer correctly identified that internal consistency cannot be deflected by frame-of-reference objections, and the V6 author structurally promoted that argument class. The hole-set this c2 round surfaces is sub-hierarchy: placement WITHIN Part 2 (Holes 02-03), strong SCs ABSENT from Part 2's structural arc (Hole 04), and tier-system blind spots in Part 1.5-1.9 (Hole 01). Macrostructure right; sub-hierarchy needs a polishing pass.
why_it_doesnt_save_the_argument: Macrostructure right is not the same as hierarchy correct. A reader who internalizes the tier framework expects ALL Tier-1 arguments in Parts 2/2b, but Part 1.5/1.7/1.8/1.9 host Tier-1-class attacks the tier framework does not advertise. Within Part 2, the foundation argument (V13 self-referential loop, currently 2.9) is buried at the bottom; the post-Summary loose arguments are hierarchy-orphaned; and the verdict-count vs subsection-count gap (20 SC WINs vs 9 named + 3 loose = 12 Part 2 derivations) reveals 8 strong SCs invisible in the structural arc. The c1 review demanded promotion of SCs; this c2 round demands the SC section's internal arc be coherent.

proposed_integration_patches.replacement_html_block:
2.13 The κ Coupling Constant: Divide-by-Zero with the Dome's Own Later Data

The dome's structural coupling constant κ = 1.67 nT/µGal is computed as a ratio of two numbers — magnetic perturbation (BOU 2017) divided by gravity perturbation (Mohe 1997). The dome's own subsequent eclipse data (WIN-013 Membach 1999, WIN-014 China 2009) report zero µGal at superconducting-gravimeter precision. A coupling constant whose denominator is the dome's own later null measurement is undefined — and this same κ is the coefficient that drives the dome's marquee August 2026 eclipse prediction (PRED-TIER-3).

The structural problem. κ is the dome's own published magnetic-gravity coupling, derived from a single eclipse pair: −10.9 nT (BOU magnetometer, 2017 North-American eclipse, ~92% local coverage) divided by −6.5 µGal (Mohe spring gravimeter, March 1997 eclipse, Wuhan-region observation). The ratio is 1.67 nT/µGal — a number the dome carries forward as a fundamental geometric coefficient and uses to predict future eclipse signatures.

The dome's own later data nullifies the denominator. Both WIN-013 (1999 Membach total eclipse, superconducting-gravimeter null at 0.01–0.1 µGal precision) and WIN-014 (2009 East-Asian eclipse, Chinese SG network null at comparable precision) are listed by the dome as confirmations. The dome simultaneously claims (WIN-012) that the eclipse gravity perturbation is 6.5 µGal and (WIN-013/014) that the eclipse gravity perturbation is 0 µGal — at higher-precision instruments at longer-duration events. A coupling constant whose denominator the dome's own data sets to zero is mathematically undefined; treating it as 1.67 nT/µGal is treating 1/0 as a finite number.

The retreat to "spatial heterogeneity" reopens the wound elsewhere. A defender will say: κ applies only where the dome predicts a signal, and WIN-013/014 are at "non-discriminating" locations. But the dome never publishes a discrimination rule. If κ is a real coupling constant of the cavity, it should not turn off and on depending on station latitude. If it does turn off and on, the dome owes a stated location-dependent function — which would constitute new model surface area subject to its own subsequent falsification at each new eclipse. And critically: if κ can be "off" at Membach 1999 and "on" at BOU 2017, then κ can also be "off" at the August 2026 prediction site — destroying PRED-TIER-3, the dome's marquee forward eclipse claim. The defense saves the past at the cost of the future.

Why this is a self-contradiction, not a fitting failure. The standard external complaint against κ is that the two data points used in its derivation are from different events, different locations, different instruments, and one of them (Mohe 1997) has not been independently replicated. That criticism is valid but external. The criticism here is internal: the dome's own later confirmations contain a contradiction with the denominator of its own foundational constant. No external instrument is required — only the dome's own published WIN-012/013/014 numbers compared against each other. The 6.5 µGal denominator and the 0.0 µGal confirmations cannot both be true at the precision the dome itself cites. Tier-1 attack: the foundational coefficient of the dome's forward gravity prediction is invalidated by the dome's own later success-list. See WIN-012, WIN-013, WIN-014, and Part 6.14 κ Coupling Cluster cross-eclipse argument for the full derivation chain.

proposed_integration_patches.replacement_html_block:
2.14 The 1,660:1 Ring-Magnet vs. Fitted-Equation Mismatch

The dome's V51 architecture is topologically a ring magnet: aetheric flux exits the Axis Mundi, traverses the disc, loops through the sump, returns. Flux conservation in any closed magnetic circuit forces near-equal pole strengths. The dome's own ring geometry predicts Bsouth ≈ 39 nT; the dome's fitted equation gives 64,852 nT — a 1,660:1 mismatch between the dome's topology and the dome's own curve-fit.

The geometric prediction. WIN-053 establishes that the V51 aetheric routing — Axis Mundi out, disc traversal, Antarctic barrier descent at r ≈ 20,015 km, sub-terrestrial return through the Sump — is a closed circuit. Closed magnetic circuits obey flux conservation Φ = B·A around the loop. The Axis Mundi aperture (estimated radius ~500 km, generously ~1,000 km) has area Apole ≈ 3.1 × 106 km²; the Antarctic-barrier circumferential band (r = 20,015 km, height ≈ firmament thickness) has area Asouth many orders of magnitude larger. Conservation demands Bsouth/Bpole = Apole/Asouth — yielding a southern-pole field of order 39 nT under the dome's own ring geometry.

The dome's fitted equation contradicts the dome's topology. The dome publishes a fitted B(r) decay equation that produces Bsouth ≈ 64,852 nT at the Antarctic barrier — a value 1,660 times larger than the dome's own ring topology allows. Either the topology is wrong (no closed ring; one-end-open monopole reverting to the dome's rejected V50.6 model) or the fitted equation is wrong (curve-fit to observations, not derived from dome geometry). The dome cannot claim both.

The exponential form is also incompatible. Beyond the magnitude mismatch, the dome's exponential decay form contradicts standard EM for any closed magnetic geometry. A ring or toroidal field with continuous flux conservation produces 1/r-type decay (or 1/r² in spherically symmetric cases), not exponential. The exponential form is a phenomenological fit to the radial gradient of the geomagnetic field at the dome-mapped latitudes — it is not derived from any ring-magnet calculation. Standard electromagnetism (Maxwell's equations, applied to the dome's own claimed topology) forbids the dome's own fitted curve.

Why this is foundational. The dome's V51 marketing centers on the two-pole geomagnetic architecture as an improvement over V50.6's monopole. Recognizing that a single pole cannot give a dipolar field shows real physical reasoning — this is the kernel of truth in WIN-053. But the execution fails on the dome's own terms: the ring topology and the fitted decay equation disagree by a factor of 1,660. The dome's own published numbers cannot both stand. See WIN-053 for the full flux-conservation derivation and the published 64,852 nT fit.

proposed_integration_patches.replacement_html_block:
2.15 Version-Unstable Geometry: FSF V50.6 → V51 Shift

The Firmament Scaling Function is claimed to be derived from V12 geometry — meaning the same formula on the same station should give the same number across dome versions. Running the formula on station HAD under V51 parameters gives FSF = 1.47; running the same formula on the same station under V50.6 parameters gives FSF = 1.89. A 28 % change in a single station's "geometric" output when other parameters drift is a curve-fit, not a geometry.

The claim. WIN-044 asserts that the Firmament Scaling Function (FSF) is derived from V12 dome geometry — a stable, parameter-independent geometric relationship that converts a station's radial position into a dimensionless scaling factor. A genuine geometric derivation should be invariant under changes to unrelated parameters: if the formula is truly geometry, then revising the firmament height profile or the sun altitude should not move a station's FSF value.

The test. Apply the same FSF formula to the same station (Hartland, HAD) under two successive published parameter sets: V51 (ellipse H(r), V51 station radii) and V50.6 (exponential H(r), V50.6 station radii). Result: HAD FSF = 1.471 under V51 vs. HAD FSF = 1.89 under V50.6 — a 28 % shift. The BOU reference radius that anchors the ratio moves from 5,715 km (V50.6) to 9,983 km (V51) — itself a 75 % change in a number nominally fixed by dome geometry.

Why this is a self-contradiction, not just parameter drift. The dome could fairly say "V50.6 was an early version; V51 is more accurate" — that is normal scientific revision. But the V12 derivation claim is the structural assertion that FSF is geometry, not fit. A formula whose output for one station changes by 28 % when adjacent parameters drift is responsive to the curve, not to the geometry. Either FSF was always parameter-fitted (in which case the V12-derivation provenance is misleading) or every prior version was using a "wrong" geometry that happened to produce different FSF values for the same physical station — which means the per-station FSF agreement the dome cites as confirmation has been moving as the parameters moved, falsifying the stable-geometric-derivation claim. The dome cannot simultaneously claim V12-derivation and 28 % version-shift.

Cross-link to coordinate collapse. The instability is consistent with the broader pattern in Part 1.8 (Coordinate Collapse): the dome's coordinate parameters move under their own refraction fix, and any quantity built atop those coordinates inherits the motion. FSF is one such derived quantity. See WIN-044 for the full version-comparison and reproduction commands.

proposed_integration_patches.replacement_html_insertion: A note on argument abundance. The case below is overdetermined: any one of the {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} self-contradictions in Part 2 alone falsifies the model, and the External Falsification Tests in Part 7 provide independent confirmation from data the dome did not choose. The argument hierarchy that follows is organized for reader navigation, not because the conclusion needs every argument. A reader who reads only the Tier-1 self-contradictions in Parts 2 and 2b finds the model already refutes itself; the lower tiers exist for completeness and to engage the dome's own framing of "kill-shot binary tests."

anticipated_objections.objection: Adding three new SC subsections inflates the count from 12 → 15, which a defender will call goalpost-moving — 'every time the dome rebuts something, the review just finds three more attacks.'
anticipated_objections.response: The three new subsections (κ divide-by-zero, ring-magnet 1,660:1, FSF version-unstable) are not new attacks discovered during this c2 round — they are existing WIN-level findings (WIN-012/013/014, WIN-053, WIN-044) that have been in data/wins.json since their respective onboarding runs and have always been tagged verdict=Self-Contradicted. P1-P3 surface them into the Part 2 structural arc; they do not add new analytical content. The count moves from 12 (named-in-Part-2) to 15 (named-in-Part-2) but the underlying SC verdict count in wins.json is unchanged at 20. If anything, the Part 2 / wins.json gap shrinks (was 12/20 = 60% surfaced; becomes 15/20 = 75% surfaced) — the structural arc gets more honest, not more inflated.

anticipated_objections.objection: P4 (V13 promotion) renumbers nine subsections — high churn for a navigation improvement that may not be noticed by most readers.
anticipated_objections.response: Acknowledged — flagged in P4 itself as operator-choice-required. The lighter alternative (add a strength-emphasis sentence to the existing 2.9 V13 intro) salvages most of the signal without the renumbering churn. Decider can choose. If P4 is deferred, P0-P3 still apply unchanged because they anchor on the post-Summary blocks and on insertion-after-2.12, not on V13's identity.

anticipated_objections.objection: P5 (Tier 1 = Parts 1.5+1.7+1.8+1.9 + 2 + 2b) makes the tier label longer and less crisp. A reader scanning the framing paragraph may bounce off the bullet list.
anticipated_objections.response: The longer-and-less-crisp framing is exactly the cost of honest tier classification. The current state is the worst of both: Part 1.5-1.9 contain Tier-1-class attacks but the tier-system advertises only Parts 2/2b as Tier 1. Either we move 1.5-1.9 into Part 1c (curmudgeon's option b — bigger restructure) or we update the tier framing to include them (option a — taken here). Option a is lower-cost; the framing-paragraph length penalty is real but smaller than the cost of leaving Tier-1-class attacks tier-orphaned.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not just add the new 2.13/2.14/2.15 subsections without renumbering the post-Summary args (skip P0)?
anticipated_objections.response: Because the post-Summary loose args are already orphaned (reader-exit-at-Summary signal) and adding 2.13-2.15 between Summary and the loose args makes the orphaning worse (Summary at offset ~39k, then 2.13/2.14/2.15, then unnumbered post-Summary args — even more confusing). The coordinated P0+P1-3 sequence has the Summary at true end-of-Part-2 with 2.10-2.15 visible structure, which is structurally cleanest. If only P1-P3 land without P0, the post-Summary arrangement gets worse, not better.

anticipated_objections.objection: The advocate_mode 'case is overdetermined' acknowledgement (P6) concedes to the defender's 'you're nitpicking a slam-dunk' framing. Does this weaken the rest of the review?
anticipated_objections.response: It concedes only on hierarchy-is-for-navigation-not-necessity — which is the curmudgeon's own framing. The substantive attacks in Parts 2-9 remain unchanged. The acknowledgement is a calibrated concession that preempts a real defender rebuttal; refusing to make it leaves the rebuttal live and unaddressed. The curmudgeon's preemptive_recommendation explicitly identified this as the right move.

summary_for_decider: EXP-450 addresses HOL-HIERARCHY c2 (7 holes — 4 major, 2 moderate, 1 minor) with six structured integration patches and three deferred items. Six patches in priority order: P0 (renumber post-Summary args 2.10-2.12 + move Summary to true end), P1-P3 (add new SC subsections 2.13 κ-divide-by-zero, 2.14 ring-magnet 1,660:1, 2.15 FSF version-unstable — surfacing WIN-012/013/014, WIN-053, WIN-044 into Part 2 structural arc), P4 (promote V13 to 2.1 — flagged operator-choice-required, lighter alternative offered), P5 (update Part 1 tier-classification to include 1.5/1.7/1.8/1.9 as Tier 1), P6 (Part 1.1 'case is overdetermined' acknowledgement per advocate_mode preemptive_recommendation). Three deferred items: HIER-PHASE2-05 callout boxes (follow-on verification-class M1 after P5 lands); HIER-PHASE2-06 Kill-Shot label rename (operator decision); HIER-PHASE2-07 within-Part-2 ranking (substantially addressed by P0+P4). Declaring review_class='holistic' because the bundle spans multiple Part 2 subsections and changes the Summary TLDR count claim, requiring a coordinated curmudgeon adversarial pass before publish rather than narrow verification. Close ISS-2273..2279 after the bundle integrates.
